reasoning: O-2-naphthyl chlorothioformate is a thioformate ester where the sulfur bears a chlorine (it is a chlorothioformate, i.e., an acyl chloride of a thioformic acid ester). The compound has an O-aryl (naphthyl) thioester linkage, best classified as a thioester derivative under thiocarboxylic acids and derivatives. The closest available direct parent label is 'Thioesters'.
